title: viola quercetorum
intro: Viola quercetorum, also known as the Oak-leaved Violet or Oak Leaf Violet, is a perennial flowering plant native to Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, due to its active compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. In herbal preparations, Viola quercetorum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat a range of health issues.
